mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance increases the cost of capital for equities, compressing forward equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 4.5% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of months where PAYEMS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) decline by more than 2% month-over-month while simultaneously the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, the joint signal indicates demand destruction and labor market stress that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% MoM AND UNRATE rises > 0.3pp within 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 724131 million dollars, UNRATE at 4.2% as of 2025-04-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the joint signal, or the interaction effect (measured as coefficient on RSAFS×UNRATE interaction term) is not statistically significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index over 2+ consecutive months signals weakening supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing activity, reducing corporate profitability and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 58% of periods where INDPRO declines for 2+ consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1400 thousand units annualized and remain elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the signal indicates robust residential demand and forward economic activity that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1400 thousand units sustained for 2+ months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1324 thousand units as of 2025-03-15
regime_hint: expansion phase with strong housing demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where HOUST > 1400 for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 320.5 index points while the federal funds rate remains below 4.5%, the real interest rate becomes negative, signaling monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equities and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 320.321, FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of 2025-04-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative real rates, or the interaction coefficient (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) is not statistically significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP (GDPC1) growth decelerates to below 2% annualized rate while unemployment remains below 4.5%, the signal indicates a late-cycle slowdown with tight labor markets that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices recession probability upward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23526.085 billion chained 2017 dollars, UNRATE at 4.2% as of 2025-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant at p < 0.10 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% and remains elevated for 3+ consecutive months, the signal indicates labor market deterioration that reduces household income and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations compress on lower earnings expectations.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances where UNRATE > 4.5% for 3+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
